options.name: --database
options.description: OBSOLETE: Please specify the connection string instead

options.name: --connectionstring
options.description: You now provide an entire connection string. ServerName and Database are obsolete.

options.name: --sqlfilesdirectory
options.description: The directory where your SQL scripts are [default: .]

options.name: --outputPath
options.description: This is where everything related to the migration is stored. This includes any backups, all items that ran, permission dumps, logs, etc. [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-grate-2.0.5-9176bc1149474c23a75bbe9ae05dc716/xdg-data/grate]

options.name: --folders
options.description:
Folder configuration.
If you wish to override any of the default folder names, supply a semicolon separated list of mappings.
You can also specify a file name that has the same contents as you would supply on the command line,
if you find it cumbersome to write all the folders on the command line.
Example:

options.name: --folders
options.description: or

options.name: --folders
options.description:
,and put the following content in `myfolderconfig.txt`(either semicolon or newline separated)
up=ddl
views=projections
beforemigration=preparefordeploy
this will keep the default folder configuration, but change the names of the folders you supply.
If you want to fully customise the folders, you can do this by specifying a list of folders with keys
that are not among the default folders. Then none of the default folders will be configured, just the ones you supply.
Example:

options.name: --folders
options.description:
The properties you can set per folder, are:
* Name - the key/name you wish to give to the folder (doesn't matter if path is specified)
* Path - the relative path of the folder, relative to the --sqlfilesdirectory parameter.
Defaults to the name given above.
* Type - the type of the migration (Once, EveryTime, Anytime), defaults to Once,
* ConnectionType - whether to run on the default connection, or on the admin (defaults to Default),
Allowed values: default, admin
* TransactionHandling - whether to be part of the transaction, or run the script even on a rollback,
defaults to Default
Allowed values: default, autonomous
There are also short forms, if you just wish to supply the folder name or the migration type.
Example:

options.name: --folders
options.description: or

options.name: --folders
options.description: the last one will expect the folders to be named 'folder1', 'folder2', and 'folder3', in the sqlfilesdirectory.

options.name: --servername
options.description: OBSOLETE: Please specify the connection string instead.

options.name: --adminconnectionstring
options.description: The connection string for connecting to master, if you want to create the database.  Defaults to the same as --connstring.

options.name: --accesstoken
options.description: Access token to be used for logging in to SQL Server / Azure SQL Database.

options.name: --commandtimeout
options.description: This is the timeout when commands are run. This is not for admin commands or restore. [default: 60]

options.name: --admincommandtimeout
options.description: This is the timeout when administration commands are run (except for restore, which has its own). [default: 300]

options.name: --databasetype
options.description: TELLS GRATE WHAT TYPE OF DATABASE IT IS RUNNING ON. [default: SQLServer]

options.name: --transaction
options.description: Run the migration in a transaction

options.name: --environment
options.description: Environment Name - This allows grate to be environment aware and only run scripts that are in a particular environment based on the name of the script.  'something.ENV.LOCAL.sql' would only be run if --env=LOCAL was set.

options.name: --schemaname
options.description: The schema to use for the migration tables [default: grate]

options.name: --noninteractive
options.description: Silent - tells grate not to ask for any input when it runs.

options.name: --repositorypath
options.description: Repository Path - The repository. A string that can be anything. Used to track versioning along with the version. Defaults to NULL.

options.name: --version
options.description: Database Version - specify the version of the current migration directly on the command line.

options.name: --drop
options.description: Drop - This instructs grate to remove the target database.  Unlike RoundhousE grate will continue to run the migration scripts after the drop.

options.name: --createdatabase
options.description: Create - This instructs grate to create the target database if it does not exist.  Defaults to true.  Set to false to emulate the --donotcreatedatabase flag in roundhouse. [default: True]

options.name: --disabletokenreplacement
options.description: Tokens - This instructs grate to not perform token replacement ({{somename}}). Defaults to false.

options.name: --warnononetimescriptchanges
options.description: WarnOnOneTimeScriptChanges - Instructs grate to execute changed one time scripts(DDL / DML in Up folder) that have previously been run against the database instead of failing.  A warning is logged for each one time script that is rerun. Defaults to false.

options.name: --warnandignoreononetimescriptchanges
options.description: WarnAndIgnoreOnOneTimeScriptChanges - Instructs grate to ignore and update the hash of changed one time scripts (DDL/DML in Up folder) that have previously been run against the database instead of failing. A warning is logged for each one time scripts that is rerun. Defaults to false.

options.name: --usertokens
options.description: User Tokens - Allows grate to perform token replacement on custom tokens ({{my_token}}). Set as a key=value pair, eg '--ut=my_token=myvalue'. Can be specified multiple times.

options.name: --donotstorescriptsruntext
options.description: DoNotStoreScriptsRunText - This instructs grate to not store the full script text in the database. Defaults to false.

options.name: --baseline
options.description: Baseline - This instructs grate to mark the scripts as run, but not to actually run anything against the database. Use this option if you already have scripts that have been run through other means (and BEFORE you start the new ones).

options.name: --runallanytimescripts
options.description: RunAllAnyTimeScripts - This instructs grate to run any time scripts every time it is run even if they haven't changed. Defaults to false.

options.name: --dryrun
options.description: DryRun - This instructs grate to log what would have run, but not to actually run anything against the database.  Use this option if you are trying to figure out what grate is going to do.

options.name: --restore
options.description: Restore - This instructs grate where to get the backed up database file. Defaults to NULL.

options.name: --searchallsubdirectoriesinsteadoftraverse
options.description: IgnoreDirectoryNames - By default, scripts are ordered by relative path including subdirectories. This option searches subdirectories, but order is based on filename alone.

options.name: --uptodatecheck
options.description: Outputs whether the database is up to date or not (whether any non-everytime scripts would be run)

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Verbosity level (as defined here: https://docs.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel)

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information
